scope.goal: Map consumers of auth-controller shadow dwords +0x1BC, +0x1C0, and +0x1C4 after the 0x140152F10 snapshot path.

working_names.auth_controller_plus_0x1bc: one-shot shadow export of non-selector-1 status +0x16C
working_names.auth_controller_plus_0x1c0: one-shot shadow export of auth sentinel/index +0x174
working_names.auth_controller_plus_0x1c4: one-shot shadow export of auth export dword +0x178
working_names.handler_0x1401f5150: shadow-export triple push and clear handler

shadow_export_handler_0x1401f5150.role: Pushes the three shadow dwords to the interpreter dword stack, then clears the shadow slots.
entry_shape: The auth controller is loaded from [RCX+0x198D0].
The dword stack base is RDX+0x100 and depth/cursor is context+0x10A0.
shadow_export_handler_0x1401f5150.interpretation: The three shadow fields are not durable controller state. They are a queued/latched result triple consumed by client-script selector 0x10E and immediately reset after export.

status_reset_handler_0x1401f5100.role: Resets live route status and source-object state when the auth controller is idle.
entry_shape: The auth controller is loaded from [RCX+0x198D0].
The handler only runs the reset path when auth_controller+0x10 is zero.
reset_effects: 0x1401F513F marks [source_object+0x78]+0x234 = 1 when the child pointer is present.
status_reset_handler_0x1401f5100.interpretation: This handler resets the live selector-specific status fields and source object, not the latched shadow triple. It matches the earlier selector-2/reset helper shape where consumed status sources are reset to idle.

constructor_setup_observations: No +0x1C0 constructor hit was found in the focused 0x140151BE0 setup scan; +0x1C0 is explicitly reset to -1 by the 0x10E shadow-export handler after consumption.

relationship_to_snapshot_0x140152f10.consumer: The 0x10E handler exports the latched triple to the interpreter dword stack and then clears the latch.
relationship_to_snapshot_0x140152f10.conclusion: The native client has both live fields and one-shot shadow fields. Scripts can read the live dwords individually through prior mapped handlers, or read the latched triple through selector 0x10E.

opennxt_947_impact: OpenNXT/client parity tests should treat +0x1BC/+0x1C0/+0x1C4 as one-shot latched auth exports, not long-lived status values.

After a script consumes selector 0x10E, the client clears the latched status to -2 and the two companion values to -1. Server-side replay or harness code should not expect the same triple to remain readable.
The live selector-specific route status fields remain +0x16C for non-selector-1 and +0x1B8 for selector 1; handler 0x798 resets both to -2 when the controller is idle.
This reduces ambiguity around the previous +0x178 unknown: even before its writer is named, its shadow export path is real and consumed as the middle value of the 0x10E triple.
next_targets: Find the script-side caller(s) of selector 0x10E to name the latched triple in clientscript terms.
Continue searching for the auth-local writer of +0x178, which is now known to feed both direct opcode 0x875 and the middle value of selector 0x10E.
Map consumers of handler selector 0x798 in JS5 scripts to identify when the client resets live auth status/source state.
